feed.title: Christopher Alberto — Blog
feed.subtitle: Notes on WordPress engineering, WooCommerce builds, and shipping for the modern web.

entry.title: Composing Clarity: How Sage View Composers Keep Your Data out of Your Templates
entry.summary: If you’ve ever found yourself wrestling with a WordPress theme that feels less like a codebase and more like a spaghetti monster made of `WP_Query` loops…

entry.summary: It’s no secret that the web is getting heavier.

entry.title: The Performance Edge: Ditching Page Builders for Custom WordPress Code
entry.summary: As a developer, I’ve seen the WordPress landscape evolve dramatically.

entry.title: Why Every Custom Post Type I Ship Now Comes With a Migration File
entry.summary: Two years into a client project, I renamed a post meta key from `_price_override` to `_manual_price_override` to make the intent clearer, pushed it, and…

entry.title: The Pest Test That Saved a Client's Checkout Flow
entry.summary: A client's checkout flow silently started charging the wrong shipping rate for three days before anyone noticed, because a "quick fix" to a custom pricing…

entry.title: Automating WordPress Deploys: My Journey from FTP to GitHub Actions and WP-CLI
entry.summary: I remember the dread of a Friday afternoon deploy.

entry.summary: I've wrestled with more WordPress themes than I care to count, and the persistent issue is always the same: getting data into templates without turning…

entry.title: My FlyntWP Pattern for Truly Reusable CTA Components
entry.summary: One of the most common headaches I used to run into on WordPress projects was the ubiquitous “Call to Action” block.

entry.title: Locking Down a Custom REST Endpoint: The Auth Mistake I Made So You Don't Have To
entry.summary: I found out a custom REST endpoint I'd built for a client's mobile app was wide open to the public internet when a routine log review turned up a request from…

entry.title: Sculpting Maintainable WordPress: Why Sage is My Go-To for Clean Code
entry.summary: I've been building WordPress sites for years, and I've seen firsthand how quickly a project can spiral into an unmaintainable mess.

entry.title: Taming WordPress Bloat: Why I Choose FlyntWP for Lean, Lightning-Fast Builds
entry.summary: I've been in the WordPress trenches for a long time, and like many of you, I’ve wrestled with the promise and the peril of page builders.

entry.title: Beyond Drag-and-Drop: Why I Choose Custom Code for WordPress
entry.summary: When diving into WordPress, the allure of page builders like Elementor or Divi is undeniable.

entry.title: Building Modern WordPress: Why I Chose Sage Over Page Builder Bloat
entry.summary: For years, the WordPress development landscape felt like a compromise.

entry.title: FlyntWP: My Go-To for Building Lightning-Fast, Bloat-Free WordPress Sites
entry.summary: I’ve spent years in the WordPress development trenches, and like many of you, I’ve wrestled with the promise and frustration of low-code page builders.

entry.title: Ditch the Drag-and-Drop: Why Custom WordPress Themes Reign Supreme
entry.summary: There’s no denying the immediate allure of a WordPress page builder.

entry.title: Decoupling WordPress: Building a Headless Frontend with Next.js and Automated CI/CD
entry.summary: We often think of WordPress as a monolithic platform, a backend and frontend tightly coupled, dictating how our content is presented.

entry.title: Reclaim Your WordPress: Why Custom Code Obliterates Bloated Builders
entry.summary: I’ve seen it countless times: a client needs a WordPress site, and the quickest path seems to be a drag-and-drop builder like Elementor or Divi.

entry.title: Ditch the Bloat: Why Vanilla WordPress, Custom Blocks, and `$wpdb` are Your Only Path to Enterprise-Grade Performance
entry.summary: Let's cut through the marketing fluff and get brutally honest about WordPress development.
